zh.Paston, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可柏斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de la Barre於大約1279年出生在英國賀福爾時，他的父親Walter de la Barre31歲，母親Matilda Devereux24歲。他於大約1302年與Mrs. Thomas de la Barre在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。他去世於1343年在英國英格蘭賀福爾享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾。

zh-hans.Paston, Norfolk, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可柏斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas de la Barre于大约1279年出生在英国賀福爾时，他的父亲Walter de la Barre31岁，母亲Matilda Devereux24岁。他于大约1302年与Mrs. Thomas de la Barre在英国賀福爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。他去世于1343年在英国英格兰賀福爾享年65岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Barre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile, Pierre, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Henri, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Regean, Yves.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (of obscure origin, possibly akin to the Celtic element barr ‘height’); or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labarre .
French: probably also a habitational name from Barre in Lozère, named with the Celtic element barr ‘height’.

variants.name: Labarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alain, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Fernande, Gilles, Herve, Laure, Michel, Urbain.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (see Barre 1), with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labar , Labare , Labarr , Labor , Labore , Lebar , and Lebarre .
History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jacques Genest or Genet dit Labarre from France (see Genest ).

variants.name: Baray
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): unexplained.
In some cases possibly also French: variant of Barray, itself a variant of Barrey, a derivative of Barre 1. In France, it is found mainly in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Barrere
meanings: French (Barrère): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrère, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.
English: variant of Barrer, for someone who lived by a town or castle gate, from Middle English barre ‘barrier, gateway’. Compare above and Barr 4.

variants.name: Barriere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gaston, Monique, Philippe.

French (Barrière): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrière, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Gates .

variants.name: Barreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Fresnel, Gabrielle, Jean-Louis, Jean-Marie, Jeanty, Michel, Michelene, Pierre.
French:

derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1), hence a nickname for a fence keeper or a topographic name for someone who lived in a house encircled by a fence.

variants.name: Barrier
meanings: French: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Old French barier, an agent derivative of bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1).
Altered form of French   Barriere  .
Altered form of German   Berger  .

variants.name: Barra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marina, Antonio, Augusto, Biagio, Carlo, Domenic, Flavio, Gennaro, Lorenzo, Luciano, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Diego, Enrique, Jaime, Juan, Manuel.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from barra ‘bar, rod’ (in the sense of ‘barrier, sandbank’), or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word.
French (southern): dialect variant of   Barre   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de la Barre was born about 1279, in Herefordshire, England, his father, Walter de la Barre, was 31 and his mother, Matilda Devereux, was 24. He married Mrs. Thomas de la Barre about 1302, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. He died in 1343, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de la Barre est né environ 1279, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de la Barre, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Matilda Devereux, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Thomas de la Barre environ 1302, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Il est décédé en 1343, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas de la Barre um 1279 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de la Barre 31 und seine Mutter Matilda Devereux 24. Er hat um 1302 in Herefordshire, England Mrs. Thomas de la Barre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne. Er starb 1343 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de la Barre è nato il circa 1279 nel Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Walter de la Barre, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Devereux, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Thomas de la Barre circa 1302, nel Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli. Morì nel 1343, nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Barreが約1279年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Walter de la Barreは31歳，母Matilda Devereuxは24歳であった。約1302年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでMrs. Thomas de la Barreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼は1343年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Barre(이)가 대략 1279년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de la Barre(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Devereux(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1302년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 Mrs. Thomas de la Barre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1343년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Mrs. Thomas de la Barre aproximadamente 1302, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1343, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de la Barre родился около 1279 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Walter de la Barre было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Matilda Devereux, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Thomas de la Barre около 1302 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Он умер в 1343 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de la Barre nació aproximadamente 1279, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de la Barre, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Matilda Devereux, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Mrs. Thomas de la Barre aproximadamente 1302, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos. Murió en 1343, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
